verdict: STOP — mismatch confirmed, no figures or policy solves generated

recipe_used.description: Exact recipe specified in the task instructions, reproduced from tools/run_intergen_bequest_exit_chain.py's own public interface
recipe_used.call_sequence: active,fixed,mechanism = arm_contract(args); base = common_overrides(args, mechanism); theta = load_theta(results.json, 'M5'); theta.update(fixed); run_model_cp_dt({**base, **theta}, verbose=False)

verified_identical_to_record.theta_dict: all 15 keys match output/.../report/results.json winners.M5.theta exactly (visually diffed)
verified_identical_to_record.mechanism_dict: bequest_spec=linear_child_scale, normalize_bequest_utility=True, owner_ltv_taper=False, owner_ltv_terminal_share=0.0, use_age_survival=True, entry_wealth_censor_to_frontier=True -- matches M5 arm_contract branch exactly
verified_identical_to_record.target_set: candidate_replacement_income_disciplined_v1 via target_system(), 15 targets, matches target_fit_full.csv moment names exactly

reproducibility_within_this_machine.conclusion: bit-identical across two runs with different NUMBA_NUM_THREADS settings -> deterministic and reproducible on this machine; the mismatch vs. the recorded artifact is systematic, not run-to-run noise

hypotheses_ruled_out: wrong theta values (visually verified identical to results.json)
wrong mechanism/arm flags (printed and verified identical to M5 arm_contract spec)

max_iter_eq passed into base_overrides() affecting anything beyond the pass-through key (read calibration.py:1188-1214; the only max_iter_eq usage is the literal dict key, which the outer common_overrides() call always overwrites with int(args.max_iter_eq) regardless of the internal base_overrides() call's argument -- confirmed the two-stage 'search-then-patch' recipe used by the production chain script is mathematically equivalent to the one-stage direct-(40,2.5e-5) recipe used here for these two keys)

stale/regenerated data files feeding overrides (income_process_overrides() and external_entry_wealth_overrides_1824() are both pure functions over hardcoded numpy literal arrays / closed-form Rouwenhorst math -- no file I/O, no RNG)
module-level warm-start/cache state in run_model_cp_dt (read solver.py:987ff -- fresh setup_parameters() + apply_overrides() every call, no persistent global state found)
within-machine numba thread nondeterminism (ruled out by the bit-identical repeat above)

leading_hypothesis.summary: Cross-platform floating-point non-reproducibility between this local macOS .venv and the Torch cluster environment that actually produced the M5 winner, amplified through a discrete-choice/kinked fixed-point equilibrium solve.
evidence: Local .venv: numpy 1.24.3, numba 0.58.1, numpy linked against MKL from /Users/tommasodesanto/miniconda3/lib, SIMD baseline SSE/SSE2/SSE3 with AVX2/FMA3 found, no AVX512.

M5 production launch script code/cluster/submit_intergen_income_disciplined_recalibration.sh runs `module load anaconda3/2025.06` on the Torch cluster -- a different anaconda distribution/version than this local .venv, with unknown (likely different) numpy/numba/MKL build and CPU SIMD path (cluster nodes may support AVX512).

intergen_housing_fertility/kernels.py has multiple @njit(cache=True, parallel=True) kernels using prange (lines ~487, ~752, ~885, ~1056) -- exactly the code shape where different SIMD widths / thread scheduling change floating-point summation order without being a correctness bug.

The equilibrium price search (scalar_market_refine, Brent's method, tol_eq=2.5e-5) is a deliberately loose research tolerance, not machine epsilon; both the recorded artifact (residual 1.48e-05) and this local solve (residual 9.32e-06) independently satisfy that same tolerance at DIFFERENT price/allocation points -- consistent with a fixed point that is not perfectly unique to floating-point precision under a kinked discrete-choice model.

Precedent check: this repo's established 'tight repeat, bit-identical' protocol (see CALIBRATION_STATUS.md e.g. July 14/15/16 entries) has only ever been validated same-machine / same-Torch-job (both repeats inside one running process on one node). There is no prior instance in project history of validating 1e-8 cross-machine (local-vs-cluster) reproduction of a Torch-computed winner.
leading_hypothesis.magnitude: All 15 moments differ by 1e-5 to 1e-2 in level (max relative difference ~1.7%, on prime30_55_childless_owner_minus_renter_mean_rooms and young_childless_renter_liquid_wealth_to_annual_gross_income_2535); loss 8.998969 (local) vs 9.044422 (recorded), a 0.5% difference; both are 'strict_converged' under the same tol_eq.

not_yet_done_due_to_time_budget: Did not obtain the exact Torch anaconda3/2025.06 package versions (would require cluster access; SSH to torch failed separately -- see main report) to make the version-mismatch diagnosis fully conclusive rather than well-evidenced-but-circumstantial.

Did not attempt a bisection (e.g. re-run with AVX512 disabled via NUMBA_CPU_FEATURES, or force numba parallel=False globally) to directly confirm the SIMD/parallel-reduction mechanism, since that risks burning the remaining time budget on a diagnostic that would not change the STOP verdict either way.
